description_long: This object consists of a rectangular solar panel mounted on a tall, slender metal pole supported by a tripod base. Above the solar panel, an antenna extends horizontally, suggesting the device is designed for communication or monitoring purposes. The solar panel features a grid of blue photovoltaic cells encased in a metallic frame, while the pole and tripod are constructed from tubular metal. A small, box-like enclosure is attached to the rear of the panel, likely housing electronic components or batteries. The overall structure is utilitarian and robust, suitable for outdoor urban environments such as streets, parks, or construction sites. The design prioritizes stability, weather resistance, and autonomous operation.
description: A tripod-mounted solar panel with an antenna and control box, designed for outdoor electronic monitoring or communication. The structure is metallic and robust, suitable for urban environments.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full solar panel, antenna, and tripod base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile of the solar panel and the attached control box.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the solar panel and the control box mounted centrally.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the pole, tripod, and control box.
